zh.lifeSummary: Joan Middlemore是Richard Middlemore的女兒，於大約1446年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾。她於大約1458年與Thomas Brome在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾結婚。她去世於大約1515年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾韋斯頓享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Middlemore是Richard Middlemore的女儿，于大约1446年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾。她于大约1458年与Thomas Brome在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾结婚。她去世于大约1515年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾韋斯頓享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Middlemore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from an unidentified place in the West Midlands, possibly the Middelmore which is recorded in the 13th and 15th centuries at Haughton in Morville (Shrops), though early bearers of the surname seem to be concentrated in Warwicks.

variants.name: Millmore
meanings: English: unexplained; probably a locative name from an unidentified minor place called Millmoor or Middlemoor. Compare the surname Middlemore , which is well recorded in Warwicks, the early bearers of which may provide some continuity with the 1501 bearer in Worcs cited below.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: Joan Middlemore was born about 1446, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Richard Middlemore. She married Thomas Brome about 1458, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. She died about 1515, in Weston, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Middlemore родилась около 1446 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали Richard Middlemore. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Brome около 1458 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла около 1515 г. в местности вестон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год.
